html:
Logah Technology (3593 TW): Distressed Platform, Not Liquid Cooling

The prior liquid-cooling/CDU angle should be discarded. Logah’s disclosed business is precision molds, plastic and metal structural parts, electronic materials, and outsourced assembly, with customers across PCs, networking, consumer devices, e-mobility, and displays. Its own 2025 investor materials describe a shift from simple manufacturing toward “design, processing, and solution integration,” plus Vietnam expansion, GaN partner exposure, AI server peripherals, semiconductor equipment parts, and smart-building services.

This is not a clean quality compounder. It is a distressed Taiwan micro-cap with full-delivery trading status, weak liquidity, no current earnings, and a very real dilution overhang. Q1 2026 revenue fell to NT$133.0 million, gross margin was negative 10.2%, operating loss was NT$41.8 million, and EPS was -NT$0.70. April revenue improved sequentially to NT$75.0 million, but was still down 51.1% year over year, with 4-month revenue down 47.2%. That is the bear case in one sentence.

The variant perception is that the ugly financials are backward-looking while the capital structure and operating perimeter are being rebuilt. Logah completed a 40% capital reduction to offset accumulated losses, is pursuing private placement shares for debt repayment, working capital, and new investments, and is restructuring Suzhou Longdeng, a negative-equity, loss-making subsidiary. The Suzhou transaction values the underlying land and factory at RMB180 million before net liabilities, while the equity transaction price was initially only RMB26.8 million because of liabilities. The key point is not “hidden asset value” in the simplistic sense, but that Logah is trying to remove a balance-sheet drag while preserving production through affiliated operating arrangements.

The torque comes from three things. First, the equity is tiny, roughly NT$1.1 billion market cap at the recent NT$18 to NT$19 quote, so even small incremental profit matters. Second, the company already has real manufacturing assets: roughly 60,000 square meters of China factory space, 122 molding machines from 50T to 2,800T, tooling capacity, and customer references including ASUS, Pegatron, Compal, Lenovo, Arcadyan, WNC, Xiaomi, BOE, Foxconn, Belkin, and others. Third, the new strategic direction is broad but potentially valuable: northern Vietnam manufacturing for supply-chain de-risking, GaN RF and power-module partner exposure through 易達通科技, semiconductor peripheral parts, AI server peripherals, and a 100%-owned smart-building integration subsidiary.

The stock is not cheap on current book. That is important. Post-reduction book value is only about NT$4.79 per share, so the old “0.5x book” argument is stale and wrong. The stock is cheap only if 2026 to 2027 is a transition year, the private placement comes with credible strategic capital, Suzhou de-risking reduces finance costs, and revenue returns toward NT$1.2 billion to NT$1.6 billion with normalized gross margin in the low teens. In that case, Logah can move from current losses to NT$50 million to NT$100 million of net income, making today’s market cap a low-teens to low-20s multiple on recovered earnings, before giving credit for any higher-margin GaN, semiconductor, smart-building, or Vietnam work.

Retail context matters. This is a thin Taiwan special-situation name, not an institutional compounder. It has recently traded under attention/full-delivery conditions, average daily volume is small, margin financing is essentially absent, and the stock has already moved sharply from its 2025 lows. That setup cuts both ways: the same float and neglect that make the stock dangerous also mean a credible strategic placement, removal of full-delivery status, or a few months of revenue and margin improvement could move the equity far more than the fundamentals alone would imply.

The cleanest thesis is: Logah is a recapitalization and control-change platform masquerading as a broken electronics-components supplier. The market sees losses, dilution, and complexity. The bull sees a listed shell with operating assets, customer access, new capital, a cleaned-up China liability structure, Vietnam optionality, and multiple small paths back to relevance. This is not a “safe” thesis. It is an asymmetric one.

Using a current stock price of about NT$18.95:

Bear target: NT$8, down 58%. Q1 weakness persists, April’s sequential rebound fails, private placement is dilutive without strategic value, Suzhou cleanup drags on, and the stock falls back toward 1.5x stressed book value.

Base target: NT$28, up 48%. The company completes the balance-sheet repair, keeps revenue around NT$1.1 billion to NT$1.3 billion, returns to break-even or modest profitability, and the market values Logah as a viable turnaround platform rather than a going-concern risk.

Bull target: NT$50, up 164%. Strategic capital comes in at tolerable pricing, Vietnam and higher-value product lines scale, gross margin normalizes into the low-to-mid teens, annual revenue approaches NT$1.6 billion to NT$2.0 billion, and EPS power reaches NT$2-plus on a fully diluted basis. At that point the stock can trade on recovered forward earnings, not distressed book value.
